title: Babbelas Bunny Chow Bobotie

seo.title: Babbelas Bunny Chow Bobotie - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • 1 large onion, cut into thin wedges
  • 30 ml sunflower oil
  • 400 g bought packet of 'Cook-in' curry sauce such as Rogan Josh or Vindaloo sauce
  • bread rolls
  • melted butter
  • 4 – 6 portions leftover bobotie
  • whole coriander leaves
  • chutney
  • tomato sambal made from small blocks of tomato, cucumber and onion and seasoned with chopped chilli, salt, white pepper, sugar and vinegar

Method:

Fry the onion in the oil until golden brown.

Add the bought curry sauce and let it simmer slowly for 5 minutes. Stir occasionally.

Hollow out the rolls and lightly brush the insides with the melted butter.

Bake for 6 – 8 minutes in a moderate oven until crisp and warm.

Heat up the leftover bobotie and fill the warm rolls with it.

Spoon some of the hot sauce over, garnish with whole sprigs of coriander leaves.

Serves 4 – 6 with chutney and tomato sambal

Easy!
